description: GLM-arbitration ablation QA set. Each query targets factual content in a scanned/rasterized academic PDF (mineru_paper.pdf, an accelerometer energy-expenditure study) whose body text MinerU OCR'd with words joined together (missing spaces). The no_gate corpus is MinerU's raw markdown (run-on text); the gated corpus is the same markdown after the online DocGate GLM visual arbitration re-inserted word boundaries on flagged blocks. A retrieved chunk is relevant if its normalized text (lowercased, alphanumerics only, after stripping HTML/LaTeX) contains ANY answer_norm string. Because normalize() removes spaces, the SAME answer matches in both corpora; any Hit@k difference is therefore purely retrieval (embedding) quality on run-on vs. de-joined text, not an artifact of the relevance test.

queries.query: What were the study purposes regarding energy expenditure prediction models from accelerometer data?
How many children participated in the study and what percentage were boys?
Which four prediction models were compared, including the mixed linear model and random forest?
On how many different wear positions were the accelerometers placed on the preschoolers?
What cross-validation scheme was used to evaluate prediction accuracy?
What semi-structured protocol did the children complete during data collection?
What was concluded about the random forest as an alternative to linear and ANN models for EE prediction?
Why do highly structured laboratory protocols tend to overestimate energy expenditure in children?
